First stable release. ShipLog is a read-only update advisor: right in Unraid's native Docker tab it shows what changed between your running image and the newest one, how risky the jump is, and the release notes — before you press update. It never updates, pulls, or stops anything.

## Highlights

- **Changelog control on every container** in the native Docker tab — a small log glyph, "Changelog", and a risk traffic-light dot. Containers with an update show what changed and how risky; up-to-date containers show the release notes of the version they're running.
- **Click-to-open bubble** with the version jump, a deterministic risk badge (digest/patch = low, minor = medium, major = high, unknown = grey), the raw changelog, the last release date, and a **DEPRECATED** badge when the upstream GitHub repo is archived.
- **Real versions, not hashes** — resolves the newest tag (following registry tag-list pagination) and shows real version numbers; the GitHub button always links to the repo's main page.
- **Optional AI summaries** via a local Ollama and **Matrix notifications** on new updates — each off by default, with a live "Check" button in the settings.
- **Read-only by construction** — the Docker socket is mounted read-only; ShipLog can't start, stop, recreate, or pull anything.
- **Settings page with cards**; the bubble follows Unraid's light/dark theme and language (DE/EN).

## How it's delivered

A single slim Unraid plugin: it bundles the Go engine (run as a host daemon — no separate container) and injects the control + bubble into the Docker tab via a same-origin proxy. The same engine image also runs as a container on any Docker host via its status page.

## Install

Unraid → **Plugins → Install Plugin**:

```
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junkerderprovinz/shiplog/main/plugin/shiplog.plg
```

Then open **Settings → ShipLog** and (recommended) set a GitHub token so changelogs resolve for more images; optionally configure Ollama and Matrix.
